title: Rock Show
weakest_section: 05:00-06:00 range with George dinner date setup

jokes.text: Your boobs are dead.
jokes.setup: Andy getting his casts removed, should be a celebratory moment
jokes.punchline: Andy makes an inappropriate comment about someone's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate inappropriate comment, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Wow, it's like a sweaty piñata.
jokes.setup: Andy's cast being removed
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disgusting comparison to describe the cast removal
jokes.explanation: Vivid, unexpected metaphor that's both gross and funny. Great economy.

jokes.text: Hey, my iPod! Oh, yeah! My pirate!
jokes.setup: Items falling out of Andy's cast
jokes.punchline: Andy had random objects including a 'pirate' stored in his cast
jokes.explanation: Reveals Andy's childlike nature and lack of hygiene awareness

jokes.text: Andy immediately falling over when trying to walk
jokes.setup: Andy excited about walking again, declares 'It is gonna feel so good to walk again, finally'
jokes.punchline: He immediately collapses when trying to walk
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with immediate physical comedy payoff

jokes.text: Sandwich! Are you turkey or ham?
jokes.setup: Context that Andy writes songs about things physically near him
jokes.punchline: He's literally singing to a sandwich
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Andy's childlike creativity and literal thinking

jokes.text: Lamp! Wish you were a lamp that would light up when you get touched
jokes.setup: Continuation of Andy singing to objects
jokes.punchline: He's singing to a lamp about wanting it to be... a lamp
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent absurdity - he wants a lamp to function like a lamp

jokes.text: You know... I don't really like to define it but it's like Matchbox Twenty meets The Fray. So, rock. Well, again, I don't really like to define it.
jokes.setup: Andy claims he doesn't like to define his music
jokes.punchline: He defines it exactly, then when called 'rock' insists again he doesn't like to define it
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Andy's pretentious yet clueless nature about his generic music

jokes.text: Oh, you guys. You know how I love mixing work into my personal life.
jokes.setup: Leslie is known for being obsessed with work
jokes.punchline: She sarcastically claims she loves mixing work and personal life when she actually does it constantly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - sarcastic about her own well-known workaholic tendencies

jokes.text: Apparently, what I can achieve in government can literally be measured.
jokes.setup: Mark got a speed bump lowered 2 inches
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation about the measurably small nature of government achievements
jokes.explanation: Sharp, cynical observation about bureaucratic limitations with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: It's one of those rare songs that rocks really hard, and also informs people about a small public works project.
jokes.setup: Describing Andy's song about the pit
jokes.punchline: The absurd combination of rocking hard and municipal information
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of rock music with mundane government topics

jokes.text: The band has had a few different names over the years... [long list ending with] ...and now we are Scarecrow Boat. God, when I hear myself say Scarecrow Boat out loud, I kind of hate it.
jokes.setup: Andy listing band names
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long list of terrible band names, ending with self-aware criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating list joke with great band names like 'Penis Pendulum' and 'Just the Tip,' topped with self-awareness

jokes.text: Not my kids, 'cause they're grown-up, but it was tough on their kids.
jokes.setup: George talking about how his divorce was tough on the kids
jokes.punchline: Reveals he means his adult children's kids, not his own children
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that also emphasizes George's age inappropriateness

jokes.text: And she looks like a young Sandy Duncan.
jokes.setup: George describing Leslie positively
jokes.punchline: Compares her to Sandy Duncan, an actress from the 1970s
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate reference for George that emphasizes the generational gap

jokes.text: You don't have to have sex with him if you don't want to.
jokes.setup: Leslie's mom trying to convince her to finish the date
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions sex as if it were expected
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable parental boundary-crossing that generates cringe laughs

jokes.text: My ex-wife Tammy cheated on me. Then we divorced. Then last week, I ran into her sister Beth here. Turns out she hates Tammy, too, so we've started dating. It's like a fairy tale.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his dating situation
jokes.punchline: Calls revenge dating his ex-wife's sister a 'fairy tale'
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with the 'fairy tale' description of petty revenge

jokes.text: Look at how hot she is. Isn't that crazy? And she's a surgeon. She makes a ton of money! Bam!
jokes.setup: Tom introducing his wife
jokes.punchline: Bragging about her attractiveness and money in front of her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - shallow and inappropriate but delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: He's like the gayest person I've ever met. But I make out with him when I'm drunk sometimes.
jokes.setup: April describing Derek
jokes.punchline: Contradicts herself immediately with the drunk makeout admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - deadpan delivery of uncomfortable personal information

jokes.text: Apparently, tonight, I'm the Parks Department's seventh wheel.
jokes.setup: Leslie observing everyone else is paired up
jokes.punchline: Uses 'seventh wheel' instead of the common 'third wheel'
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on common phrase that emphasizes how left out she feels

jokes.text: Just a taste. Have a little taste of chicken! Just a bit! Just a taste! Chicken, chicken, chicken!
jokes.setup: George offering Leslie chicken
jokes.punchline: His increasingly aggressive and weird insistence
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation that makes George seem increasingly inappropriate

jokes.text: Could you grab me a triple whiskey water?
jokes.setup: Andy asking Ann to get him a drink
jokes.punchline: Orders an absurdly strong drink with water
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's immaturity and drinking inexperience

jokes.text: The last rock show I went to was the Everly Brothers.
jokes.setup: George saying he hasn't been to a rock show in a long time
jokes.punchline: The Everly Brothers are not remotely rock music
jokes.explanation: Good generation gap joke showing George's disconnect from actual rock music

jokes.text: Ann [sung repeatedly]
jokes.setup: Andy dedicating a song to his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: The entire song is just her name repeated
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly simple joke showing Andy's lack of songwriting ability while being weirdly sweet

jokes.text: They should change their name to Big, Lying Baby. Or Where's My Sandwich? Or Don't Forget To Bring Home Beers After Work.
jokes.setup: Discussing the band's name
jokes.punchline: Ann suggests increasingly passive-aggressive names that reference Andy's behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating joke that reveals Ann's frustration through increasingly specific suggestions

jokes.text: But if I were, would you be interested? No. Okay.
jokes.setup: Mark insisting he's not hitting on Ann
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks if she'd be interested, gets shot down
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Mark's inability to take no for an answer

jokes.text: Pit! I fell in it, the pit / You fell in it, the pit / We all fell in it, the pit
jokes.setup: Andy's song about the pit incident
jokes.punchline: Incredibly simple, repetitive lyrics about falling in the pit
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Andy's limited songwriting ability while being genuinely catchy

jokes.text: No! Screw it! We are Mouse Rat!
jokes.setup: Andy announcing the band name as Scarecrow Boat
jokes.punchline: Mid-announcement he changes back to Mouse Rat
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Andy's impulsiveness and indecision

jokes.text: Todd graduated in 2005, so you probably missed him.
jokes.setup: Tom asking if George knows Todd from Indiana State
jokes.punchline: 37-year gap between graduations
jokes.explanation: Simple age gap joke delivered with Tom's characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Dead or asleep?
jokes.setup: George appears unconscious
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact question about his condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that perfectly capture the situation

jokes.text: She used to read him books at the senior center. He's six, but he has Benjamin Button's disease.
jokes.setup: Question about Leslie's 'boyfriend'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation involving Benjamin Button disease
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke with absurd pop culture reference that actually makes sense

jokes.text: You should be dating men that Al Roker announces their birthdays.
jokes.setup: Mark joking about Leslie dating older men
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific reference to Today Show centenarian birthday announcements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, specific pop culture reference that perfectly captures dating someone extremely old

jokes.text: That time we were also at a bar. And he was also pretty drunk.
jokes.setup: Leslie comparing current moment to past hookup with Mark
jokes.punchline: The only similarities are bar + drunk Mark
jokes.explanation: Good awkward observation about their past encounter and current situation

jokes.text: You think I can get my beer bottle in that shopping cart right there?
jokes.setup: Mark suggesting throwing trash in the pit
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified reaction since she wants to build a park there
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Mark's cynicism vs Leslie's idealism

jokes.text: You missed the entire pit, which is huge, by the way.
jokes.setup: Leslie throwing her bottle
jokes.punchline: Despite the pit's size, she completely missed it
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy callback emphasizing the pit's prominence

jokes.text: I really, really like it when you serve me food.
jokes.setup: Andy finally admitting the truth about keeping his casts on
jokes.punchline: He enjoyed being waited on hand and foot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - selfish but delivered with childlike honesty

jokes.text: Look, I mean, you got Dirt Slide over here, you got Ring Around The Diaper you could play there. You've got Duck, Duck, Glass you could play right there. Mystery Trash.
jokes.setup: Mark sarcastically describing the pit as already being a park
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and dangerous versions of children's games
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list of twisted children's games that perfectly captures the pit's current state

jokes.text: I mean, Kennedy put a man on the moon. I can build one park.
jokes.setup: Leslie getting motivated despite obstacles
jokes.punchline: Compares building a park to the moon landing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her eternal optimism and grandiose perspective

jokes.text: This isn't the way I want this to happen again.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Mark about to hook up again
jokes.punchline: She says 'again' while claiming she doesn't want it to happen
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's self-awareness and tendency to overthink

jokes.text: Mark falling into the pit
jokes.setup: Mark being cynical about the pit becoming a park
jokes.punchline: He literally falls into the very pit he was dismissing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - the pit literally claims another victim after Mark dismissed its danger

jokes.text: Pit! I fell in it, the pit / You fell in it, the pit / We all fell in it, the pit
jokes.setup: Mark just fell in the pit
jokes.punchline: Andy's song plays as the credits roll, now literally true for Mark too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms Andy's silly song into prophetic truth - perfect episode ending

tmdb_overview: Leslie attends what she believes is a business meeting with an elderly local government bigwig, unaware that her mother has secretly set her up on a blind date. Andy plays his first gig since breaking both of his legs in the pit.
insight: Early season finds its footing: character comedy and cringe sustain a thin plot.
